description: Lock basic_cartography to the sixth-century BCE Babylonian World Map artifact and prevent generic later cartographic methods from reappearing as direct prerequisites.

checks.reason: Cuneiform labels, distance annotations, reverse-side descriptions, and the colophon are integral parts of the surviving map.
The specifically scoped artifact cannot retain its documented mapped identities and explanatory structure without writing.
The museum evidence does not document land-survey measurements or a surveying workflow for the artifact.
The Babylon-centered cosmography is not documented as a navigational chart or as derived from navigation observations.
The artifact record does not establish a geometric construction or projection method.
Written distance annotations do not establish mathematical calculation as the map's production method.
The surviving tablet is approximately dated to the sixth century BCE.
